message:
fix(exec): replace bounded sync_channel with unbounded channel for subprocess I/O

The subprocess stream pumping code used sync_channel(1024) / sync_channel(128)
which blocks the writer thread when the buffer fills. If a child process
produces output faster than the consumer drains it (e.g., during a large
build or test run), the child process stalls waiting for buffer space,
causing apparent hangs and timeouts.

Switching to unbounded mpsc::channel() lets the pump threads drain the
child's stdout/stderr pipes without blocking, buffering in memory instead.
This trades bounded memory for guaranteed forward progress — the consumer
still processes frames in order, but the child process never stalls on
a full channel.

Affected sites:
- extension_dispatcher: exec streaming (SyncSender → Sender)
- extension_dispatcher: bash exec streaming (SyncSender → Sender)
- extensions: hostcall exec ref streaming (SyncSender → Sender)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
